resource.note: only 1 monitored bed left — pediatric resp distress should claim it

time.target: immediate placement for SpO2 91% + accessory muscle use

warnings: monitored bed scarcity — 1 free with multiple 'now' tier patients in queue
child unable to speak full sentences = impending respiratory failure risk

hypotheses.name: Acute asthma exacerbation (moderate-to-severe)
supporting_signals: known asthma history with 4 prior visits (last for exacerbation)
RR 32, HR 132, SpO2 91% on room air
already used home albuterol x2 with inadequate response

hypotheses.name: Impending respiratory failure
rules_against: no cyanosis or altered mental status reported

hypotheses.name: Lower respiratory infection (pneumonia/viral) triggering flare
rules_against: no cough or focal findings noted

immediate.action: Place on supplemental O2 and continuous SpO2/cardiac monitoring; move to monitored bed now
immediate.reason: SpO2 91% with accessory muscle use meets pediatric respiratory distress criteria.

immediate.action: Albuterol nebulizer (weight-based) + ipratropium; consider continuous neb if no improvement; oral/IV corticosteroid
immediate.reason: Wheezing pediatric asthma flare unresponsive to 2 home inhaler doses.

immediate.action: MD bedside evaluation now; prepare for escalation (Mg sulfate, epi IM, BiPAP) if no improvement after first neb
immediate.reason: Short-phrase speech and SpO2 91% indicate moderate-to-severe flare with risk of decompensation.

monitor.action: Reassess RR, SpO2, work of breathing, and air entry after each neb (q15-20 min)
monitor.reason: Track treatment response and detect deterioration early.

monitor.action: Consider CXR if no improvement, asymmetric breath sounds, or first-time wheeze concern; pulse ox trending
monitor.reason: Imaging only if response inadequate or focal findings emerge.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Notify pediatrics / PICU early given monitored-bed scarcity and severity
escalate_or_redirect.reason: If response to nebs is poor, child will need admission/PICU; bed planning should start now.

explanation.summary: 6-year-old with known asthma in moderate-to-severe exacerbation: SpO2 91%, RR 32, HR 132, accessory muscle use, fragmented speech, inadequate response to 2 home albuterol doses. Meets pediatric respiratory distress criteria on multiple axes (hypoxia + accessory muscles + speech limitation). Tier: now (ESI 2, with potential to escalate to ESI 1).

citations.signal: SpO2 91% + accessory muscle use + unable to speak full sentences
high-risk pediatric respiratory presentation with abnormal vitals

checklist.task: Move to monitored bed and start supplemental O2
MD bedside assessment and steroid order
Reassess vitals + work of breathing post-neb
Pediatrics/PICU consult if poor response after 2 nebs
Bed-flow check — only 1 monitored bed free with multiple 'now' patients

why_this_tier: Accessory muscle use + short-phrase speech triggers R-PEDS-RESP-01
Failed home rescue inhaler x2 indicates moderate-to-severe flare
would_downgrade_if: SpO2 ≥95% on room air after first neb
Resolution of accessory muscle use and full-sentence speech
would_upgrade_if: SpO2 drops further or fails to rise with O2
Silent chest, altered mental status, or rising pCO2
Need for BiPAP, IM epinephrine, or intubation
